_notes: Auto-generated by scripts/classify_districts.py (Stage 4) produced an EMPTY rule set: the pack's scraped breadcrumbs are only 2 levels deep (chapter 'Unified Development Ordinance' -> article, e.g. 'Zoning Districts'), with no division-level breakdown, so Stage 4's article-level grouping lumped all 32 individual base-district sections (R-2 ... PD-MC) into one heterogeneous 'Zoning Districts' bucket and correctly refused to assign it a single district. Human-reviewed 2026-07-18: added per-section title_contains rules for each of the 32 'Zoning Districts' article sections (each section title unambiguously names exactly one district, e.g. Sec. 72-31.2 'R-2 Residential District.', confirmed against the ordinance body text and the city's live ArcGIS 'Zoning' layer district codes) plus a rule for the citywide Sec. 72-40.1/72-40.2 Use Table (Use Standards article), which lists all base + PD district codes as columns and is relevant to every mapped district. Residential districts (R-2, R-4, R-8, R-12, R-16, R-30, R-MH) all collapse to 'residential-low-density' -- the closed DISTRICT_VOCAB has no separate high-density residential tier, same as every other pack. Commercial/office districts (C-T, C-D, C-SC, C-H) and Planned Development-Commercial (PD-C) -> 'commercial-employment'. Industrial districts (I-1, I-2) -> 'industrial-zone'. Planned Development-Residential (PD-R) -> 'residential-low-density'; Planned Development-Mixed-Use (PD-MU) -> 'mixed-use-core' (both have real, GIS-verified on-the-ground parcels). One flagged judgment call: Planned Development-Medical Center (PD-MC, Sec. 72-33.4) mapped to 'commercial-employment' -- its purpose text describes an employment-center campus of hospital + medical offices, not zero-employee institutional use, so it reads as a commercial-employment district rather than 'unknown', but there is no PD-MC parcel in the on-the-ground GIS layer to round-trip-verify, so no dataset/fixture scenario relies on it. Left unmapped (unknown/general), matching the 'don't force overlay/general-standards articles into the vocab' precedent from henrico-county-va: (1) Sec. 72-30.1-72-30.6 (general provisions ABOUT the districts article, not a district itself); (2) Sec. 72-32.7 Creative Maker District -- its entire body text is 'The Creative Maker District is governed by Appendix 72-A, which is incorporated into this chapter', and Appendix 72-A was never scraped into this pack, so there is no ordinance text to ground a classification even though the GIS layer confirms a 'CM' code exists on the ground; (3) Sec. 72-34.1, 72-34.3-72-34.9 (Old and Historic Fredericksburg / Floodplain / Wetlands / Chesapeake Bay Preservation / Gateway Corridor / Neighborhood Commercial / Special Tourism and Events / Technology overlay districts -- these layer on top of a base district rather than being one); (4) the 'Use Standards' article's per-use-category sections (Sec. 72-41.x Residential/Institutional/Commercial/Light manufacturing/Industrial/Utility uses, Sec. 72-42.x accessory uses, Sec. 72-43.x permit procedure, Sec. 72-44.1) and the entire 'Development Standards' article (subdivision, streets, parking, utilities, landscaping, fences, lighting, signs) -- these are genuinely cross-district standards organized by use/topic, not by district, matching how henrico left its equivalent Article 4/5 unmapped. Rules are first-match.
